data.title: Trump pressures Israel to slow down attacks on Lebanon
data.description: To prevent Israeli military action in Lebanon from disrupting upcoming US-Iran negotiations, US President Trump has asked Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu to reduce the intensity of the military strikes against Lebanon. Reports indicate that Trump spoke with Netanyahu and stated "sternly" that the US understands Israel's security concerns, but Israel should slow its military operations in Lebanon to maintain the upcoming negotiations. A US official revealed that the call was "shorter than usual." The official stated that the Trump administration had never previously asked Israel to slow its military operations against Hezbollah in Lebanon. This pressure stems from accusations by Iran and mediator Pakistan that Israel has violated the ceasefire agreement; Trump fears that continued strikes will affect the US-Iran ceasefire and disrupt the opening of the Strait of Hormuz. (CCTV News)

data.title: Oil Futures Steady as U.S. and Iran Plan Talks on Middle East Ceasefire
data.description: Oil futures prices remained relatively stable as traders prepared for the weekend, with upcoming talks between the United States and Iran potentially influencing the continuation of the Middle East ceasefire. According to Odaily, Scott Shelton from TP ICAP noted that traders have largely exited the market, with recent price fluctuations occurring with minimal human trader involvement. These movements were primarily driven by necessary hedging or position adjustments to further reduce risk exposure. Shelton added that the weekend discussions might provide clarity on whether the differences between Iran and the U.S. are too significant to reach an agreement.

data.title: Bitcoin Transaction Fees Drop Significantly Over the Year
data.description: Daily Bitcoin transaction fees have decreased to 2.443 BTC as of April 8, marking a 69% decline compared to the same period last year. According to NS3.AI, the average fee per block was approximately $0.33, with fees comprising less than 0.5% of the total miner revenue.

data.title: Bitcoin Leads in Social Dominance with Ethereum and XRP Following
data.description: Bitcoin has emerged as the leader in social dominance, capturing 33.55% of the market, according to NS3.AI. Ethereum follows with an 11.85% share, while XRP holds 2.6%. In terms of AI search volume, both Solana Foundation and Ethereum recorded 11%, surpassing Aave, Polygon, and Bittensor.

data.title: Hapvida Aims to Raise $398 Million Through Southern Brazil Operations Sale
data.description: Hapvida Participacoes e Investimentos is reportedly aiming to raise up to 2 billion reais ($398 million) by selling its operations in southern Brazil. Bloomberg posted on X that the health-care corporation is looking to reduce its debt through this sale. The move comes as part of Hapvida's broader strategy to streamline its operations and improve financial stability. The company has not yet disclosed specific details about potential buyers or the timeline for the sale. This decision reflects a growing trend among corporations to optimize their portfolios and focus on core areas amid challenging economic conditions.

data.title: Analysts: With US-Iran talks imminent, oil traders are hedging their bets.
data.description: Crude oil futures prices remained largely unchanged as traders headed into the weekend, with planned talks between the US and Iran potentially determining the sustainability of the ceasefire in the Middle East. Scott Shelton of TP ICAP said, "Traders have essentially withdrawn from the market. A $7 swing like yesterday appears to have occurred with very little human intervention; they were simply hedging or clearing positions to further reduce risk exposure." He added, "Perhaps after this weekend, we'll have a clearer picture of whether the differences between Iran and the US are too great to prevent an agreement." (Jinshi)

data.title: Ripple Strategy partners with Trident to build a digital payment system based on the RLUSD stablecoin.
data.description: Ripple Strategy Holding announced a strategic partnership agreement with Nasdaq-listed Trident Digital Tech Holdings. The two companies will jointly build a digital payment system based on the Ripple USD (RLUSD) stablecoin and blockchain payment infrastructure. This system will introduce stable liquidity pools to create a low-cost, real-time settlement foreign exchange market and leverage Ripple's global payment network covering more than 90 markets to achieve efficient coordination of cross-border payments and capital flows. (Globenewswire)

data.title: Crypto stocks opened higher across the board, with Strategy& rising 1.28%.
data.description: According to data from msx.com, at the opening of the US stock market, the Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 0.03%, the S&P 500 rose 0.27%, and the Nasdaq Composite rose 0.4%. Cryptocurrency stocks generally rose, with Circle up 2.3%, Coinbase up 0.66%, Robinhood up 0.38%, and Strategy up 1.28%. msx.com is a decentralized RWA trading platform that has listed hundreds of RWA tokens, covering US stock and ETF tokens such as AAPL, AMZN, GOOGL, META, MSFT, NFLX, and NVDA.

data.title: U.S. Gasoline Prices Reach Record High Since 1967
data.description: Gasoline prices in the United States have reached their highest level since 1967, according to Jin10. This increase is reflected in the Consumer Price Index (CPI), highlighting significant inflationary pressures within the energy sector. The surge in gasoline prices is a key contributor to the overall rise in the CPI, affecting consumer spending and economic dynamics. Analysts are closely monitoring these developments as they could influence future monetary policy decisions by the Federal Reserve.

data.title: White House Predicts Energy Price Drop with Hormuz Strait Reopening
data.description: The White House National Economic Council Director, Kevin Hassett, announced that the Hormuz Strait could reopen within two months. According to Odaily, Hassett stated that once the strait is operational, energy prices are expected to decrease rapidly. The number of vessels passing through the Hormuz Strait has decreased by 10% compared to normal levels.

data.title: Citigroup Economists Estimate Core PCE Monthly Increase at 0.3%
data.description: Citigroup economists have made a preliminary estimate that the core Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) index will show a monthly increase of 0.3%, based on today's Consumer Price Index (CPI) data. According to Jin10, this figure exceeds the Federal Reserve's target of a 2% year-over-year increase but is an improvement over the 0.4% rise recorded in February. The Citigroup team noted that they will need to consider Producer Price Index (PPI) data to make a more accurate prediction for the PCE.

data.title: Annex Wealth Management Economist Discusses Impact of Rising Gasoline Prices on CPI
data.description: Annex Wealth Management's Chief Economist, Brian Jacobsen, commented on April 10 that while he anticipated a rise in overall CPI due to increasing gasoline prices, the actual data was surprising. According to Jin10, Jacobsen noted that there is currently no evidence of high energy prices affecting core inflation. He explained that this process might take time to manifest, as businesses are likely to absorb most of the initial impact. In a way, consumers cutting back on other discretionary spending could potentially lower core inflation rather than increase it.

data.title: CoinDesk 20 Experiences Slight Decline Amid Mixed Asset Performance
data.description: The CoinDesk 20 index recorded a minor decrease, trading at 2,007.93, reflecting a 0.2% drop or a decline of 3.4 points from the previous day's close. According to NS3.AI, individual asset performances varied, with HBAR experiencing a 1.9% decline and ADA falling by 1.3%. In contrast, AVAX saw a rise of 0.6%, while BTC increased by 0.3%.

data.title: The probability of the Federal Reserve keeping interest rates unchanged in April is now at 98.4%, consistent with the probability before the CPI data release.
data.description: According to CME's "FedWatch": the probability of the Federal Reserve raising interest rates by 25 basis points in April is 1.6%, and the probability of keeping rates unchanged is 98.4%. The probability of the Fed cutting rates by a cumulative 25 basis points by June is 1.5%, the probability of keeping rates unchanged is 96.8%, and the probability of raising rates by a cumulative 25 basis points is 1.7%.

data.title: Polymarket's probability that "the Strait of Hormuz will return to normal by May 31" has dropped to 43%, a 12% decrease in the last 24 hours.
data.description: According to Odaily Seer's monitoring, the probability of the Strait of Hormuz returning to normal by May 31st on Polymarket has dropped significantly, falling 6% in 1 hour and 12% in 24 hours. The contract rules for this event state that if the International Monetary Fund's Portwatch reports a 7-day moving average of vessel arrivals ("vessel arrivals") in the Strait of Hormuz equal to or greater than 60 on any date between the market's creation and May 31, 2026, the market will be rated "Yes"; otherwise, it will be rated "No". Daily vessel arrivals include container ships, bulk carriers, roll-on/roll-off ships, general cargo ships, and tankers; vessels not reported by IMF Portwatch will not be considered. Pakistani media confirmed today that an Iranian delegation has arrived for negotiations. Major talks between Iran and the United States will be held tomorrow, focusing on reopening the Strait of Hormuz and extending the ceasefire. Furthermore, the US delegation participating in the US-Iran negotiations has departed for Islamabad, Pakistan, and US Vice President Vance has boarded the plane. Before departing, Vance stated that he looked forward to negotiations on Iran, adding that US President Trump had provided "quite clear guidelines" for the talks. Odaily Seer continues to monitor the prediction market, seeing changes before pricing.

data.title: Iran has not yet formally confirmed the negotiating team or its delegation members, making the prospects for a lasting peace agreement unclear.
data.description: Officials in Pakistan's capital said an Iranian delegation will arrive in Islamabad Friday evening to prepare for key peace talks scheduled for Saturday with the United States. One anonymous source said Iranian Foreign Minister Araqchi and Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf will lead the delegation. US President Trump has sent Vice President Vance, special envoy Witkov, and son-in-law Kushner to Islamabad to meet with Iranian officials. The Iranian embassy in Islamabad did not respond to requests for comment. Iran has not yet officially confirmed its participation in the negotiations or the members of its delegation, although Iranian state media have stated that Ghalibaf will lead the talks. Given Israel's continued bombing of Hezbollah targets in Lebanon, the prospects for a lasting peace agreement this weekend remain uncertain. The US and Iran are also far apart on demands for a long-term ceasefire, with the Strait of Hormuz remaining a key sticking point. (Jinshi)

data.title: U.S.-Iran Negotiations in Islamabad May Extend for Several Days
data.description: A special envoy for Iran's Supreme Leader has indicated that ongoing negotiations between the United States and Iran in Islamabad could last for two to three days. According to NS3.AI, the discussions are currently taking place in Pakistan, with both parties engaged in dialogue to address key issues.

data.title: Market Analyst Comments on CPI: Home Goods Prices See Largest Drop Since Late 2024
data.description: Market analyst Chris Anstey has commented on the Consumer Price Index (CPI), noting that home goods prices fell by 0.2% month-on-month, marking the largest decline since late 2024. According to Jin10, this category was expected to reflect the impact of tariff effects. It is important to remember that March followed the Supreme Court's decision to invalidate most of U.S. President Donald Trump's tariffs, which were subsequently replaced by a lower 10% tax.

data.title: European Defense Stocks Decline Amid Ukraine-Russia Peace Deal Hopes
data.description: European defense stocks experienced a downturn on Friday following remarks from a senior Ukrainian official indicating advancements in peace negotiations with Russia. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the market's reaction to the potential for a ceasefire. Meanwhile, construction companies saw an uptick, driven by expectations that a peace agreement could lead to a surge in post-war reconstruction efforts. The developments have sparked optimism in the construction sector, anticipating increased demand for rebuilding infrastructure in the aftermath of the conflict.

data.title: Dollar Faces Pressure Amid US-Iran Negotiation Reports
data.description: The dollar-and-oil buying trade is experiencing challenges as reports of negotiations between the United States and Iran surface. According to NS3.AI, the US Dollar Spot Index has decreased by approximately 1.4% this week, marking its largest weekly drop since January. This decline is attributed to shifts in foreign exchange trading, influenced by headlines surrounding the negotiations and a fragile ceasefire.
